target.name: Sila Nanotechnologies
target.founder: Gleb Yushin, Alex Jacobs, Gene Berdichevsky

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $1.3B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sila Nanotechnologies is a late-stage climate and energy company that has raised $1.3B+ across multiple rounds. The comparable universe consists of 9 peers in the Climate & Energy sector: 1 public company (Linamar, ticker LNZA) and 8 private firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables at similar valuation tiers to support a reliable median multiples approach—only 0 narrow peers met filtering criteria—making conventional peer-median analysis inapplicable.

Given data constraints, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology applied to the $1.3B total capital raised as a proxy baseline. Applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Late Stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $1.6B. This method reflects the company's progression through capital rounds but does not incorporate company-specific operational or technology metrics beyond financing history.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B (floor, using raised capital directly) to $1.8B (ceiling, applying upside assumptions to the stage-progression result), with a median point of $1.6B. Key uncertainties include the lack of comparable late-stage private financings in the energy storage and materials space, unobserved revenue or unit economics, and the long development timelines typical of frontier hardware companies. A future round, operational disclosures, or acquisition of closer peer data would refine this range materially.
